name: Pecan Penuche
recipeInstructions:
1. Oil a jelly-roll pan or an 8 x 8-inch pan.
2. Combine the sugar, milk, and salt in a heavy 3-quart pot, stirring to mix well.
3. Place over medium heat and bring to a boil, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves.
4. Continue boiling, over medium heat, until the mixture reaches the firm ball stage (240-250°F), stirring only if it starts to burn.
5. Remove from the heat and immediately place the pot in a larger pot filled with cold water; this will stop the cooking process and bring the temperature down.
6. Drop in the butter and let cool slightly, without stirring.
7. Beat until the mixture starts to thicken, add the vanilla and pecans, and continue to beat until the candy loses some of its gloss.
8. Spread evenly in the pan and mark into squares.
9. When firm, cut into pieces and store in an airtight container.
